Issues: Inconsistency between description and mockups: The listing copy describes the product as 'abstract graphic assets' designed for digital use (Tech Interfaces, Web Design). However, the mockups (especially the mug and stacked cards) portray these assets as physical, printed goods. The listing must clearly specify if the buyer is purchasing a digital file (templates/icons) or a physical printed item (stationery, merchandise).

Gradient complexity for physical print: While visually appealing, selling these gradients requires careful attention to color separation and physical printing limitations (e.g., matte finish vs. gloss finish, ink limitations on certain materials like kraft card). This needs to be addressed in the material guidelines.
